description_long: This is a classic cast iron radiator with a series of evenly spaced vertical columns, each forming a fin for efficient heat dissipation. The radiator is predominantly metallic with a slightly worn, off-white painted finish, showing some signs of use and minor discoloration. On one end, there is a visible pipe assembly with two connection points and a manual valve, likely for water or steam input and output. The opposite end is plain, with no visible controls or connections. The radiator's design is utilitarian, with robust construction and a simple, functional aesthetic typical of older urban heating systems. The overall form is rectangular, with the vertical fins creating a ribbed texture across the main body.
description: A classic cast iron radiator with vertical columns, off-white paint, and visible pipe connections on one end. The design is robust and utilitarian, with a ribbed texture and manual valve.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full array of vertical fins and the pipe assembly with valve on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profile of the radiator and the depth of the vertical fins.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the vertical fins and the pipe assembly now on the right side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the narrow profile and the pipe connections and valve in detail.
attribute_pipe_connections: Two pipe connections on one end, bottom and side entry, horizontally oriented
